sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Auxerre are struggling, having not won in their last 3 matches, with a recent 2-2 draw against Stade Rennais showing some fight but defensive lapses. Le Havre are in worse shape, winless in their last 5 matches, suffering a heavy 6-2 defeat to Marseille, highlighting defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Le Havre have dominated Auxerre, winning both matches (3-1 and 2-1). Historically, their games often see both teams scoring, with 6 out of 6 recent head-to-heads having goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Auxerre (15th, 7 points) have struggled offensively with only 7 goals scored (15th in league), though they’ve overperformed expected goals by 1.97. Le Havre (16th, 6 points) have conceded 16 goals, showing defensive weakness, but have scored 10, slightly better than Auxerre.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Auxerre’s strength lies in ground duels (1st in league) and tackling (1st), which could disrupt Le Havre’s play. Le Havre, however, have a knack for late goals (25% of goals after 76th minute) and could exploit Auxerre’s tendency to concede first (5/7 matches).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (40% home win, 35% away win, 25% draw) and recent form, Auxerre have a slight edge at home, but Le Havre’s head-to-head dominance cannot be ignored. Expect a tight, competitive match with potential for both teams to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Auxerre and Le Havre ended in a goalless draw, with neither side able to break the deadlock. Le Havre dominated possession and created more dangerous opportunities, including a couple of big chances that went unconverted. Auxerre, playing at home, showed resilience in defense but struggled to create clear-cut chances in the final third. Both teams earned an equal number of corners, indicating some attacking intent, but the finishing touch was lacking from both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Le Havre showcased a stronger presence in the first half, controlling the ball for the majority of the time and making more entries into the final third and penalty area. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, gave them an edge in physical battles. Despite missing key opportunities, their attacking play looked more structured. Auxerre, on the other hand, appeared more cautious, with fewer entries into dangerous areas and a reliance on counter-attacking moments. Their defensive organization held firm, but they need to be more adventurous in the second half to turn the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Auxerre, Danny Namaso stood out with a notable shot on target and some promising involvement in the attack. Donovan Léon also impressed in goal, maintaining a clean sheet under pressure. On Le Havre’s side, Mbwana Ally Samatta was a constant threat, getting a shot on target and being involved in creating danger, though he missed significant chances. Yanis Zouaoui also played a pivotal role in setting up plays, contributing to Le Havre’s attacking efforts with a key creative spark.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum, with Auxerre starting on the front foot, pushing forward in the early minutes. However, Le Havre quickly took control, enjoying prolonged spells of pressure and mounting consistent attacks around the midway point of the half. Auxerre responded with a brief resurgence, showing intent to push back, before Le Havre reasserted dominance towards the end of the half with a particularly strong attacking spell in stoppage time. The momentum swings suggest Le Havre holds the upper hand going into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Discipline and physicality
halftimeSections.text: The game has been physical, with Le Havre picking up two yellow cards compared to Auxerre’s one. This indicates a slightly more aggressive approach from the away side, which could play a factor if tempers flare in the second half. Auxerre will need to maintain composure while matching Le Havre’s intensity to avoid being overpowered in duels, where they’ve already shown some vulnerability.
